summary:
Hydroxyapatite makes up approximately 65-70% of the inorganic content in bone according to multiple reliable sources. This consistent figure supports choice D as correct without requiring further modification based on current information provided by recent research findings.
The primary focus here was establishing a precise numerical representation supported widely within medical literature while maintaining alignment between available data and existing options presented initially regarding composition ratios specific only towards identifying which proportion correctly describes what fraction belongs specifically under 'inorganic' category pertaining strictly just skeletal structure components excluding organic elements like collagen fibers etc...
In summary, Agent concluded that hydroxyapatite comprises about 65-70% of the inorganic part of bone after reviewing several supporting documents but did not find reason to alter their original assessment favoring answer D over other possibilities due to consistency found throughout referenced materials concerning these proportions attributed solely toward mineralized tissue making up skeleton framework minus softer connective tissues involved directly during growth processes amongst others contributing factors influencing overall density/strength characteristics observed clinically speaking when examining human skeletons postmortem examinations conducted rigorously scientifically validated methodologies employed consistently yielding same results repeatedly thus lending credibility high degree accuracy expected from such specialized fields dealing heavily quantitative measurements necessary characterizing complex biological systems studied extensively through decades now leading experts universally agreeing upon core principles underlying basic anatomy physiology related topics including elemental makeup comprising major structural support system known commonly referred simply ‘bone’. 

Key Clinical Points Summarized:
Hydroxyapatite composes roughly 65-70% of bone’s inorganic material; this ratio aligns closely enough with Option D to maintain its validity despite additional review confirming initial understanding accurately reflects reality per established scientific共识。骨的无机成分主要由羟基磷灰石构成，约占总量的大约65%-70%，这一比例在多项研究中得到了一致确认，并且与选项D相符。
答案：骨的主要无机成分为羟基磷灰石，占比约为65%-70%。

总结：
羟基磷灰石占骨头总无机组分的比例为大约65%-70%，这支持了选择D作为正确答案；经过进一步审查后未发现需要改变最初评估的理由以偏向其他可能性的情况，因为这些数据在整个参考材料中有高度的一致性。 \boxed{\text{羟基磷灰石}}
